properties.remark: WIND GUST OF 52 KNOTS, OR 60 MILES PER HOUR, WAS OBSERVED AT FLORIDA KEYS MARATHON AIRPORT. THIS WIND GUST WAS PRODUCED BY A THUNDERSTORM IN A SQUALL LINE PASSING THROUGH THE MIDDLE KEYS.

THE ISLAND FISH COMPANY RESTAURANT NEAR THE ENTRANCE OF KEY COLONY BEACH HAD WINDS WHICH OVERTURN LARGE PICNIC TABLES, DAMAGED THE TIKI ROOF, AND A NEON SIGN DAMAGED. THESE WINDS WERE PRODUCED BY A THUNDERSTORM IN A SQUALL LINE CROSSING NEAR KEY COLONY BEACH.

NWS EMPLOYEE SURVEYED STORM DAMAGE ALONG THE OVERSEAS HIGHWAY FROM MILE MARKER 51 TO 59. THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED AT THE ISLAND FISH COMPANY...LOCATED AT MILE MARKER 54.5. LARGE OUTDOOR FURNITURE BECAME AIRBORNE...PEOPLE COULD NOT STAND IN THE WIND...AND THERE WAS DAMAGE TO A TIKI BAR ROOF. BASED ON THIS ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO NEARBY TREES...STRAIGHT LINE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE NEAR 70 MPH. WIDESPREAD PEA TO MARBLE SIZE HAIL WAS ALSO REPORTED IN THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS WAS OBSERVED AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS PRODUCED BY A THUNDERSTORM IN A SQUALL LINE PASSING OVER THE REEF SOUTH OF MARATHON.

A FUNNEL CLOUD WAS SPOTTED AT 848 AM EDT...SOUTH OF CUDJOE KEY MOVING WEST TOWARD AMERICAN SHOAL LIGHT. FUNNEL EXTENDED THREE-QUARTERS OF THE WAY TO THE SURFACE BEFORE A RAIN SHAFT OBSCURED SPOTTERS VIEW. VISIBLE FOR 4 MINUTES...DURATION UNKNOWN.

AT 912 AM EDT...THE CAPTAIN OF THE VESSEL...HOT TAMALE...CALLED IN A REPORT OF A WATER SPOUT SOUTH OF THE BAHIA HONDA BRIDGE...MOVING WEST-NORTHWEST TOWARDS LITTLE PALM ISLAND. SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE...BUT SPOUT WAS ONLY VISIBLE ABOUT HALF-WAY TO THE SURFACE. DURATION UNKNOWN.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATER SPOUT 2 TO 3 MILES NORTHWEST OF CRAIG KEY. THE WATER SPOUT WAS VISIBLE HALFWAY TO THE WATER...AND THERE WAS A SPRAY RING. THE WATER SPOUT HAD A DURATION OF ABOUT 10 MINUTES BEFORE DISSIPATING.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 3 MILES SOUTH OF MILE MARKER 52...OR JUST SOUTH OF MARATHON. THE WATERSPOUT WAS SEEN MOST OF THE WAY TO THE WATER. THE MOVEMENT OF THE WATERSPOUT WAS TO THE SOUTHWEST AT 10 KNOTS.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 3 MILES NORTH NORTHEAST OF MILE MARKER 82. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ABOUT THREE FORTHS OF THE WAY TO THE HORIZON. THE SPOTTER WAS UNABLE TO TELL IF THERE WAS A SPRAY RING DUE TO OBSTRUCTIONS.

AN OFF DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 15 MILES NORTH NORTHWEST OF STOCK ISLAND MOVING TO THE SOUTHWEST AT 12 MILES PER HOUR. THE WATERSPOUT WAS REACHING THE GROUND...HOWEVER...THERE WAS NO SPRAY RING VISIBLE.
A COMMERCIAL FISHERMAN REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ABOUT HALF WAY DOWN AND IT WAS MOVING TOWARD THE NORTHWEST. THERE WAS ALSO A SPRAY RING VISIBILE.

A COMMERICAL FISHERMAN REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTHEAST OF BIG PINE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS HALF OF THE WAY DOWN AND IT WAS MOVING TO THE NORTHWEST. THERE WAS ALSO A SPRAY RING VISIBLE. AT 1035 AM AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED THE SAME WATERSPOUT 2 AND A HALF MILES SOUTH OF BAHIA HONDA. THE WATERSPOUT WAS THEN ALL THE WAY TO THE GROUND. THE WATERSPOUT THEN DISIPATED SHORTLY AFTER THIS REPORT.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC JUST OFFSHORE OCEANSIDE NEAR MILE MARKER 93. A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE NEAR HIGHWAY ONE AND TAVERNIER CREEK OBSERVED THE SAME WATERSPOUT DISSIPATE AS IT CAME ASHORE. DURATION 2 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER 1 MILE WEST OF SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT...OR 7 MILES SOUTH OF MARATHON. THE VISIBLE FUNNEL WAS ABOUT 3/4 OF THE WAY TO THE WATER.

A SOUTHEASTERLY WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS...51 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ATOP OF SAND KEY LIGHT...AT 206 AM EDT. THIS GUST OCCURRED DURING A HEAVY DOWNPOUR...CAUSED BY THUNDERSTORMS PASSING NEARBY.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT OVER THE GULF WATERS NORTH OF SUGARLOAF KEY.

AT 658 AM EDT...A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATER SPOUT APPROXIMATELY 1 MILE WEST OF PLANTATION KEY...OVER THE WATERS OF FLORIDA BAY. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE EAST WITH NO TIME OF DISSIPATION INDICATED.
AT 700 AM EDT...A TRAINED SPOTTER ON DUCK KEY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT JUST SOUTHEAST OF MILE MARKER 61 ON US 1. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE EAST AND NO TIME OF DISSIPATION WAS INDICATED.

AT 724 AM EDT...A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATER SPOUT JUST SOUTHWEST OF DUCK KEY IN A LINE OF HEAVY SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS. MOVEMENT WAS TOWARD THE EAST...BEGIN TIME OF THIS SPOUT IS ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 716 AM EDT.

A WEST-NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 58 KTS...OR 67 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON LONG KEY LIGHT...AT 1045 AM EDT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM. WINDS AT THE 1100 AM OBSERVATION WERE FROM THE NORTH AT 22 KTS.

A NORTHWEST WIND GUST OF 38 KTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON LONG KEY LIGHT...AT 1146 AM EDT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM. WINDS AT THE 1200 PM OBSERVATION WERE FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHWEST AT 13 KTS.
A SOUTH-SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 43 KNOTS...OR 50 MPH...WAS REPORTED AT THE C-MAN PLATFORM ON LONG KEY LIGHT WHICH WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A SLOW MOVING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 65 KNOTS...OR 75 MPH...FROM THE WEST-NORTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE LONG KEY C-MAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A SLOW MOVING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 50 KNOTS...OR 58 MPH...FROM THE SOUTH-SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE LONG KEY LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...FROM THE WEST-NORTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSESS REEF LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A ROPE-LIKE WATERSPOUT WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING ABOUT ONE MILE NORTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE NEAR MILE MARKER 41. DURATION 2 MINUTES.

A VETERAN STORM CHASER/TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED THE COMPLETE LIFE CYCLE OF A WELL-DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF MARATHON...INCLUDING A SPRAY RING AT THE SEA SURFACE AND A SUBSEQUENT CONDENSATION FUNNEL FROM OCEAN TO CLOUD BASE. DURATION 7 MINUTES.

A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTH WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION. THE WIND GUST WAS THE RESULT OF A GUST FRONT FROM A THUNDERSTORM LOCATED IMMEDIATELY NORTH OF THE STATION.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED 3 WATERSPOUTS 4 MILES NORTH OF BIG PINE KEY. HE REPORTED THAT ONE WAS ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER AND HAD A SPRAY RING...THE SECOND WAS CLOSE TO THE WATER BUT NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING...AND THE THIRD WAS ABOUT HALF OF THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER. THE MAN WAS DRIVING UP U.S. 1 SO THERE WAS NO REPORT WHEN THE WATERSPOUTS DISSIPATED.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...FROM THE EAST-NORTHEAST WAS RECORDED BY THE NORTHWEST FLORIDA BAY USF COMPS STATION AS A THUNDERSTORM MOVED SOUTHWARD OFF THE MAINLAND MONROE COUNTY COAST.

A NORTH-NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 41 KTS...OR 47 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT AT 1117 AM EDT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM. WINDS AT THE 1200 PM OBSERVATION WERE FROM THE EAST AT 10 KTS.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS WAS REPORTED AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT.

SEVERAL TRAINED SPOTTERS AND SEVERAL MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC CALLED IN REPORTS OF NUMEROUS WATERSPOUTS NEAR CUDJOE BAY AND SUGARLOAF KEY. AS MANY AS 7 WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED AT THE SAME TIME WITH CLOSE PROXIMITY TO LAND.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS REPORTED AT THE SAND KEY CMAN SITE.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED AT THE SAND KEY CMAN STATION AT 1100 PM.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS REPORTED AT THE LONG KEY CMAN STATION AT 1126 PM.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS REPORTED AT THE MOLASSES REEF CMAN STATION AT 1256 AM.

A SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 1025 AM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM. WINDS AT THE 1100 AM OBSERVATION WERE FROM THE EAST-SOUTHEAST AT 12 KTS...OR 14 MPH.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPRTED A WATERSPOUT 2 MILES NORTHWEST OF LONG KEY BRIDGE. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER AND WAS VISIBLE FOR 17 MINUTES.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED TO BE ALL THE WAY TO THE GROUND. THE WATERSPOUT WAS VISIBLE FOR ABOUT 3 MINUTES BEFORE DISSIPATING.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT JUST TO THE NORTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE NEAR PIDGEON KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER.

TWO DEAD PINE TREES DOWN...ONE ACROSS PALM BEACH ROAD IN CENTRAL BIG PINE KEY. 6-INCH DIAMETER LIMB FROM HEALTHY BUTTONWOOD TREE ALSO DOWN. NEARBY RESIDENCE AT CWOP SITE DW1404 ON PENSACOLA ROAD RECORDED WIND GUST OUT OF THE NORTH-NORTHWEST AT 51 MPH.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC AND A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF CONTENT KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF CUDJOE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 3 WATERSPOUTS WEST OF SUGARLOAF KEY. THE FIRST WAS OBSERVED AT 810 PM...AND THE OTHER TWO WERE REPORTED AT 824 PM. THESE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED ALL THE WAY TO THE HORIZION. THEY WERE ALSO SHORT LIVED AND LASTED LAST THAN 5 MINUTES.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER/CAPTAIN WHO WAS LOCATED ABOUT 11 MILES SOUTH OF THE WEST END OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE REPORTED 3 WATERSPOUTS EXTENDING TO THE OCEAN SURFACE ALONG WITH A FOURTH FUNNEL CLOUD THAT DID NOT EXTEND TO THE SURFACE. A SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE ON THE 3 WATERSPOUTS THAT EXTENDED TO THE SURFACE.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY THE AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL TOWER AT THE KEY WEST AIRPORT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS LOCATED ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH AND MOVING WEST. DURATION WAS 6 MINUTES.

AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. A SECOND REPORT FROM THE AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL TOWER AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT REPORTED THE DURATION AT 6 MINUTES.

A WELL FORMED WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES SOUTH OF THE CITY OF LAYTON ON LONG KEY. THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED TO HAVE FORMED ON THE WEST END OF A WEST TO EAST ORIENTED LINE OF CONVECTION SOUTH OF THE UPPER KEYS. DURATION ESTIMATED AT 5 MINUTES.
A THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PRODUCED A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTHEAST AT THE LONG KEY C-MAN PLATFORM.
A NORTH NORTHWESTERLY WIND GUST OF 44 KTS...OR 51 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 542 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING LINE OF STRONG THUNDERSTORMS.
A NORTHERLY WIND GUST OF 37 KTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON LONG KEY LIGHT AT 619 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING LINE OF STRONG THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY LIGHT AT 403 AM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 407 AM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE NORTH NORTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A PASSING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A SOUTH SOUTHWESTERLY WIND GUST OF 48 KTS...OR 55 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT AT 517 PM EDT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER ON CUDJOE KEY AT 817 AM EDT.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON MOLASSES REEF LIGHT AT 911 AM. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING LINE OF THUNERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 1026 AM. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS...OR 47 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORMS IN THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 43 KNOTS...OR 49 MPH...FROM THE EAST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORMS IN THE AREA.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 7 NAUTICAL MILES NORTH-NORTHWEST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED IN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. DURATION 12 MINUTES.

SEVERAL TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTERS REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES NORTH OF SUGARLOAF KEY WHICH FORMED IN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. THE WATERSPOUT EXTENDED TO THE SEA SURFACE WITH AN EVIDENT SPRAY RING. DURATION 15 MINUTES.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT NORTH OF BIG COPPITT KEY. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED IN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. DURATION 13 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE NORTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH THE PASSAGE OF A REMNANT THUDERSTORM OUTFLOW BOUNDARY.

AT 741 AM EDT...AN OBSERVER AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT REPORTED A FUNNEL CLOUD ABOUT 7 MILES TO THE SOUTHWEST. BY 750 AM IT MATURED INTO A WATERSPOUT AND WAS MOVING WESTWARD. NWS KEY WEST PERSONNEL OBSERVED THIS WATERSPOUT IN ADDITION TO 2 DEVELOPING FUNNEL CLOUDS...WITHIN THE SAME AREA OF AN EARLY-MORNING CLOUD LINE. THE SPOUT RETREATED AT 755 AM AND ALL FUNNEL CLOUDS HAD DISSIPATED BY 807 AM. DURATION WAS ABOUT 26 MINUTES FOR THE ENTIRE EVENT.

AT 809 AM EDT...A KEY WEST NWS EMPLOYEE OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT FORMING APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. THIS SPOUT DISSIPATED AT 815 AM AND AN ADDITIONAL WATERSPOUT FORMED AT 825 AM WITHIN THE SAME AREA. THIS SECOND SPOUT DISSIPATED AT 831 AM. THESE SPOUTS WERE IN AN EARLY-MORNING CLOUD LINE EXTENDING EAST TO SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST. DURATION WAS 6 MINUTES FOR EACH SPOUT.
A WIND GUST OF 41 KNOTS...OR 47 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED BY A THUNDERSTORM THAT DEVELOPED OVER THE LOWER KEYS AND MOVED TO THE WEST.
A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY THE PUBLIC ABOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF DUCK KEY OR MILE MARKER 66.
TRAINED SPOTTER 2 MILES NNW KEY WEST SPOTTED WATERSPOUT MOVING NORTH ESTIMATED TO BE 8 TO 10 MILES NNW OF KEY WEST.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED NORTH OF GRASSY KEY AND WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED HALF WAY BETWEEN KNIGHT KEY AND SOMBERERO KEY LIGHT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER AND HAD A DURATION OF 17 MINUTES BEFORE DISSIPATING.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED 2 MILES NORTH OF CUDJOE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY TO THE WATER.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT 1 MILE NORTHWEST OF THE KEY WEST HIGH SCHOOL FOOTBALL FIELD.

*** 1 INJ *** AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A TREE THAT WAS STRUCK BY LIGHTNING AT THE WHITE STREET PEIR IN KEY WEST. ALSO...LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT A BOY WAS INJURED BY THIS LIGHTNING STRIKE.

A PARK RANGER FROM FORT JEFFERSON NATIONAL PARK REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS SOUTHEAST OF GARDEN KEY. THESE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER AND WERE ASSOCIATED WITH A STORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
TREE LIMBS DOWN ON A RESIDENTIAL STREET IN KEY HAVEN DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...40 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT A WEATHERFLOW SENSOR AT THE U.S. COAST GUARD GROUP KEY WEST IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS REPORTED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL C-MAN PLATFORM IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING OVERHEAD.
AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT EAST OF UPPER MATECUMBE KEY. DURATION OF EVENT 8 MINUTES.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.

A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN PLATFORM. THE WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM THAT FORMED OFF A NORTH-SOUTH ORIENTED OUTFLOW BOUNDARY.

A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHEAST WAS RECORDED BY PRIVATE WEATHER INSTRUMENTATION AT THE UNITED STATES COAST GUARD STATION. THE WIND GUST WAS A RESULT OF A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC AND A NWS EMPLOYEE APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED IN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS AND JUST EAST OF A SHOWER. DURATION 6 MINUTES.

AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WELL DEVELOPED WATERSPOUT EXTENDED TO THE SEA SURFACE AND FORMED IN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. DURATION 10 MINUTES.

A TRAINED WEATHER OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA KEY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT IN THE GULF WATERS ABOUT 2.5 MILES NORTHEAST OF THE AIRFIELD. THE WATERSPOUT EXTENDED TO THE SEA SURFACE AND FORMED IN A CUMULUS CLOUD LINE NORTH OF THE LOWER KEYS. DURATION 15 MINUTES.

A SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 34 KTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT AT 802 AM EDT. THIS WIND GUST WAS FROM A LINE OF HEAVY SHOWERS...WITH AN EMBEDDED THUNDERSTORM...PASSING THROUGH THE DRY TORTUGAS AND VICINITY.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM MOVING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY NAVY PERSONELL AT THE BOCA CHICA TOWER. THE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER.

AT 815 AM EDT...A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A MATURE WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE NORTHWEST OF SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. IT EXTENDED TO THE SURFACE OF THE WATER WITH A SPRAY RING VISIBLE 35 TO 40 FEET IN THE AIR. MOVEMENT APPEARED TO BE TO THE WEST. DISSIPATED AT UNKNOWN TIME...BUT A SECOND SPOTTER REPORTED IT WAS ONGOING AT 827 AM EDT.

AT 1007 AM EDT...A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER IN TAVERNIER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT ONE HALF-MILE NORTH OF MILE MARKER 93 IN THE UPPER KEYS...OVER FLORIDA BAY. MOVEMENT WAS TOWARD THE WEST AND THE SPOUT DISSIPATED AT 1009 AM. ESTIMATED DURATION WAS ABOUT 5 MINUTES.

AT 823 AM EDT...A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE AT WFO KEY WEST REPORTED WATERSPOUTS BETWEEN 2 AND 5 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF KEY WEST...MOVING WESTWARD. THE MOST DEVELOPED OF THESE SPOUTS WAS ROUGHLY 2 MILES FROM KEY WEST AND VISIBILY EXTENDED ABOUT HALF-WAY TO THE HORIZON. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE AS VIEW OF THE SURFACE WAS OBSTRUCTED. BY 835 AM ALL SPOUTS HAD DISSIPATED. DURATION ESTIMATED TO BE ABOUT 15 MINUTES.

A TRAINED WEATHER OBSERVER AT THE BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 4 MILES NNW OF THE AIRFIELD. THE CONDENSATION FUNNEL EXTENDED LESS THAN A QUARTER WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE WITH NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING. WATERSPOUT MOVEMENT WAS TO THE NORTH WITH A DURATION OF 26 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY THE AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL TOWER AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. DURATION 13 MINUTES.

A TRAINED WEATHER OBSERVER AT THE BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 4 MILES NNW OF THE AIRFIELD. THE CONDENSATION FUNNEL EXTENDED LESS THAN A QUARTER WAY DOWN FROM THE CLOUD BASE WITH NO VISIBLE SPRAY RING. WATERSPOUT MOVEMENT WAS TO THE NORTH WITH A DURATION OF 26 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY THE AIR TRAFFIC CONTROL TOWER AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. DURATION 13 MINUTES.

A COMMERICAL FISHERMAN DRIVING ALONG THE SPANISH HARBOR BRIDGE SPOTTED A WATERSPOUT IN THE AREA OF COUPON BIGHT IN SOUTH BIG PINE KEY. THE WATERSPOUT WAS VISIBLE ON THE WATER SURFACE BEFORE BEING DISSIPATED BY A NEARBY SHOWER. OBSERVED DURATION ESTIMATED AT 5 MINUTES.
A THUNDERSTORM GENERATED A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT.
A THUNDERSTORM DELIVERED A WIND GUST OF 44 KNOTS AT LONG KEY LIGHT.
A THUNDERSTORM GENERATED A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS AT LONG KEY LIGHT.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS 2 MILES SOUTH OF KEY COLONY BEACH. THE WATERSPOUTS WERE OBSERVED ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER BEFORE DISSIPATING SHORTLY AFTER.

AT 900 AM EDT THIS MORNING A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER OBSERVED TWO WATERSPOUTS NEAR LOWER MATECUMBE KEY. A LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED ABOUT 1 MILE SOUTHWEST OF THE ISLAND...WHILE A LONG AND THIN SPOUT WAS OBSERVED ABOUT 2 AND A HALF TO 3 MILES SOUTH OF THE ISLAND. DURATION FOR BOTH SPOUTS WAS APPROXIMATELY 15 MINUTES.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC TRAVELLING NORTHBOUND ALONG THE OVERSEAS HIGHWAY NEAR RAMROD KEY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF BIG PINE KEY AND EAST OF MUNSON ISLAND.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT EXTENDED TO THE OCEAN SURFACE AND WAS LOCATED OFF OF LONG BEACH IN SOUTH BIG PINE KEY. DURATION UNKNOWN.
A WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS...OR 48 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM PASSING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY A C-MAN STATION AT SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM PASSING THROUGH THE AREA.

A COMMERCIAL FISHERMAN REPORTED A WATER SPOUT JUST SOUTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATER SPOUT DISSIPATED AS IT DRIFTED SLOWLY NORTHWARD TOWARDS KEY WEST. THE DURATION OF THE WATER SPOUT WAS LESS THAN 10 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER 2 MILES EAST OF NO NAME KEY. THE WATERSPOUT EXTENDED HALF OF THE WAY TO THE WATER...HOWEVER...NO SPRAY RING WAS OBSERVED.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A FUNNEL CLOUD 1 TO 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF KEY COLONY BEACH AT 937 AM EDT. THE FUNNEL CLOUD THEN EXTENDED TO THE SEA SURFACE WITH A VISIBLE SPRAY RING AT 946 AM EDT. THE WATERSPOUT DRIFTED TO THE EAST AND DISSIPATED AT 948 AM EDT.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED BY A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEE AND AN AIR TRAFFIC CONTROLLER AT THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT. THE WATERSPOUT FORMED IN A SW-NE ORIENTED LINE OF CUMULUS OVER THE GULF WATERS. DURATION 10 MINUTES.
A LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY THE ON DUTY WEATHER OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA KEY. MOVEMENT WAS TO THE EAST WITH A DURATION OF 13 MINUTES.

TRAINED SPOTTERS REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS NEAR MARATHON. THE FIRST ONE WAS OBSERVED ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE WATER WITH A SPRAY RING. THE SECOND WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED 1 MILE NORTH OF GRASSY KEY AND EXTENDED HALF OF THE WAY TO THE WEATER. THESE WATERSPOUTS WERE ASSOCIATED WITH A SHOWER PASSING THROUGH THE AREA.
CMAN STATION REPORTED A 42 KNOT THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.
A PILOT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 NAUTICAL MILES NORTH OF THE MARATHON AIRPORT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED AT FL050. DURATION UNKNOWN.
AT 420 PM EDT...A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A NARROW WATERSPOUT JUST NORTH-NORTHWEST OF CUDJOE KEY. IT WAS VISIBLE HALFWAY TO THE SURFACE BEFORE IT DISSIPATED. ESTIMATED DURATION OF ABOUT 5 MINUTES.
PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT VCNTY JOHN SAWYER BANK LIGHT, GULF SIDE MIDDLE KEYS. DURATION AT LEAST 5 MINUTES BEFORE LOSING SIGHT OF THE SPOUT.
A TRAINED OBSERVER AT THE BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION REPORTED A FUNNEL CLOUD WHICH EXTENDED A FEW HUNDRED FEET BELOW AN ISLAND CUMULUS CLOUD LINE. DURATION 8 MINUTES.
LARGE TREES SNAPPED AND POWER LINES DOWNED ALONG SOMBRERO BEACH ROAD IN MARATHON. REPORTED AT 430 AM EST TO STATE WARNING POINT FROM MONROE COUNTY COMMUNICATIONS. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
A NORTH NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...40 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A NORTH NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...44 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A NORTH NORTHEAST WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS...44 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A SOUTH WIND GUST OF 43 KNOTS...49 MPH...WAS MEASURED AT THE LONG KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION IN ASSOCIATION WITH A THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY. THIS GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM PASSING THROUGH THE AREA.
A WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS...OR 46 MPH...WAS REPORTED BY THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSAS REEF LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...OR 41 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS REPORTED AT THE PULASKI SHOAL LIGHT CMAN PLATFORM. THE WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A PASSING SQUALL LINE.

A WESTERLY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 38 KTS...OR 44 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SAND KEY LIGHT...AT 1210 PM EST. THIS WIND GUST WAS FROM A PASSING SQUALL LINE...ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING COLD FRONT.

A WESTERLY THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 36 KTS...OR 41 MPH...WAS RECORDED BY THE C-MAN STATION ON SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT...AT 147 PM EST. THIS WIND GUST WAS FROM A PASSING SQUALL LINE...ASSOCIATED WITH AN APPROACHING COLD FRONT.

A PRELIMINARY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY REPORTS AN EF0 TORNADO OVER SOUTHEAST KEY WEST AND EXTENDING INTO WEST CENTRAL STOCK ISLAND. DAMAGE REPORTS INCLUDE SEVERAL DOWNED TREES, SNAPPED PALM TREES,AND LOOSE DEBRIS BLOWN INTO POWER LINES. WIND ESTIMATES ARE IN THE 60 TO 70 MPH RANGE.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSESS REEF C-MAN PLATFORM.
A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS RECORDED AT THE MOLASSESS REEF C-MAN PLATFORM.
A WIND GUST OF 37 KNOTS...OR 43 MPH...FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS RECORDED AT THE LONG KEY C-MAN PLATFORM.
